title: hypolepis aquilinaris
intro: Hypolepis aquilinaris, also known as the fan-leaved maidenhair fern, is a type of vascular plant in the family Adiantaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling, as well as protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Hypolepis aquilinaris include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, and it can be prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the dried leaves in hot water.
